pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 1

readAloudText.Wonder: Nori Narwhal wakes as soft bubbles drift past the coral door.
readAloudText.Explore: Nori opened one eye. Bubbles floated past the coral door, round and slow, like tiny sleepy moons.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 2

readAloudText.Wonder: Puddle Otter taps the water and notices all the bubbles floating the same way.
readAloudText.Explore: Puddle tapped the water with one paw. Every bubble drifted left. “The bubble tide is moving,” he whispered.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 3

readAloudText.Wonder: Mimi Minnow worries her favorite blue bubble will float too far away.
readAloudText.Explore: Mimi Minnow swam in a tiny circle. Her favorite blue bubble bobbed near a shell, then began to drift.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 4

readAloudText.Wonder: Nori suggests following the bubbles slowly while everyone stays close.
readAloudText.Explore: Nori did not chase. He smiled gently and said, “We can follow slowly. Bubbles like calm friends.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 5

readAloudText.Wonder: The friends pass a garden of waving kelp that tickles Puddle’s nose.
readAloudText.Explore: Kelp waved from the sand. One soft ribbon tickled Puddle’s nose, and he made the tiniest underwater sneeze.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 6

readAloudText.Wonder: Mimi counts three bubbles so she can feel brave.
readAloudText.Explore: Mimi counted the nearest bubbles. One round. Two shiny. Three blue. Counting made the water feel smaller.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 7

readAloudText.Wonder: Nori uses his gentle horn glow to guide the group through a dim coral arch.
readAloudText.Explore: The coral arch was shadowy, but not scary. Nori’s tiny horn glowed like a night-light, and everyone floated through.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 8

readAloudText.Wonder: Puddle finds a shell cup where tired bubbles can rest.
readAloudText.Explore: Puddle spotted a shell shaped like a cup. A few tired bubbles nestled inside, wobbling happily.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 9

readAloudText.Wonder: Mimi’s blue bubble gets stuck under a soft sea fern.
readAloudText.Explore: The blue bubble tucked itself under a sea fern. Mimi gasped, but Nori shook his head gently. “No rushing.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 10

readAloudText.Wonder: Mimi asks for help, and the friends make a careful bubble breeze.
readAloudText.Explore: Mimi asked, “Can we help?” Nori nodded. Puddle swished one paw. Mimi swished one fin. A tiny bubble breeze began.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 11

readAloudText.Wonder: The blue bubble slips free and joins the others without popping.
readAloudText.Explore: The fern opened. The blue bubble slipped out, round and safe, and Mimi laughed so softly that it barely made a ripple.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 12

readAloudText.Wonder: The bubble tide carries the friends to a moonlit tide pool.
readAloudText.Explore: The bubbles led them to a pool where moonlight rested on the water like a silver blanket.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 13

readAloudText.Wonder: Mimi lets the blue bubble float with its bubble family.
readAloudText.Explore: Mimi watched the blue bubble drift among the others. “It looks happy,” she said, and her fins relaxed.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 14

readAloudText.Wonder: Nori shows that favorite things can be loved and allowed to move.
readAloudText.Explore: Nori touched his nose to the water. “Some things visit,” he said. “Some things float on. We can still love them.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Nori and the Bubble Tide, page 15

readAloudText.Wonder: The friends swim home slowly under a trail of sleepy bubbles.
readAloudText.Explore: Puddle yawned. Mimi smiled. Nori led the way home under a trail of bubbles, each one glowing softer than the last.
